title: Patient-advocate review checklist (plain language, reading level, no advice creep)
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual produces patient-facing health content at riskTier HIGH. A patient-advocate review checklist is required so patient advocates can systematically verify plain-language clarity, reading level (at or below grade 8), emotional appropriateness, and absence of advice creep before any source definition is signed off. The advocate reviewer must be a different person from the clinician.
objective: Author a patient-advocate review checklist covering: plain-language clarity, reading level (at or below grade 8), emotional appropriateness, absence of advice creep, and notMedicalAdvice notice. Include a sign-off recording procedure.
acceptanceCriteria: templates/advocate-review-checklist.md is created with checkable items covering: plain-language clarity (at or below grade 8 reading level), emotional appropriateness, absence of advice/instruction/dosing content, notMedicalAdvice notice present, and resolution status of all agentFlags
Checklist includes a sign-off block capturing: reviewer name, health-literacy or patient-navigation competence, date, COI declaration, and disposition of each agentFlag
Checklist notes that the advocate reviewer must be a different person from the clinical reviewer
Checklist is consistent with the review artifact data model in PLAN.md
resources: PLAN.md — Quality, review and risk gates section
